corpus: KorQuAD v1.0 dev, 113 real Korean Wikipedia paragraphs, 450-char cap applied to BOTH systems
metric: paragraph recall@1, both fully local (no API)

memvid_v1_english_control.note: same system works in English — isolates language as the variable

caveats: memvid tested = pip `memvid` v1 (video layer deprecated in v2). v2 is Rust; not run here.
v2's advertised default embedders (BGE-small/base, GTE-large) are also English-centric per its README, so the Korean gap is architectural, not v1-specific.
measures ONE axis (Korean retrieval quality). memvid's real strengths (multimodal clip/whisper, Rust/Node/Python SDKs) are not tested and are conceded.
latency is end-to-end incl. query embedding — the metric memvid's marketed 0.025ms P50 excludes (their own author cites ~5-17ms end-to-end).
